title: Schefflera tremula
intro: Schefflera tremula, also known as the 'umbrella plant', is a perennial shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Schefflera tremula contains bioactive constituents such as lignans, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Schefflera tremula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
